hooks.command: if echo "$CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT" | grep -qE 'rm -rf|rm -fr'; then echo 'BLOCKED: rm -rf not allowed' >&2 && exit 2; fi

FILE=$(echo "$CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT" | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('file_path',''))" 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$FILE" ] && echo "$FILE" | grep -qE '\.py$'; then python3 -m ruff check --fix "$FILE" 2>/dev/null || true; fi

hooks.prompt:
BEFORE STOPPING — run this checklist:
(1) VALIDATE: Was /validate (full, 4 waves) run? Check .validation_passed has waves_passed=4. If only quick (waves_passed=1), re-run /validate now.
(2) REVIEW: For any code/spec/pipeline changes — did /review run on the diff?
(3) EVALUATE: If an eval batch was launched via /eval-run — did Phase 4 analyze_eval.py complete and produce eval_summary?
(4) DASHBOARD: After any eval run OR spec count change — was the dashboard-refresher agent invoked? (visualizations/*.html hardcoded values go stale)
(5) COMMIT: Was /validate sentinel written before git commit? Pre-commit gate enforces this.
(6) COMPLETENESS: All task items finished — not just the easy ones? No partial implementations?
(7) EVIDENCE: Proof for every 'works' or 'fixed' claim — actual output, not assumption?
(8) RECORD: Docs updated in known-issues.md / CLAUDE.md for any new gotchas found?
If ANY is NO — address it. Skills to use: /validate, /review, /eval-run. Agent to use: dashboard-refresher. Don't stop until all 8 pass.
